preamble: The shift from [bʱ] to [v] represents a process of spirantization and lenition, where a voiced aspirated bilabial stop weakens into a voiced fricative (often labiodental [v] or bilabial [β]).

examples.note: In many modern Indo-Aryan languages and dialects, original voiced aspirated stops have undergone weakening, sometimes resulting in fricatives like [v] or [β].
While not from an aspirated source, Spanish shows a prototypical lenition of voiced stops to fricatives in intervocalic positions, a process analogous to the spirantization of [bʱ].

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
